geographicSummary: District Nine covers the Far East side of Columbus, from the Whitehall and Big Walnut Park borders on the west out toward Reynoldsburg, Pickerington, and Canal Winchester. The district includes established neighborhoods like Eastmoor and Walnut Ridge and has the second-largest Black population among the nine districts.

pointsOfInterest.name: Blacklick Woods Metro Park
pointsOfInterest.description: 652-acre park with beech-maple forests, buttonbush swamp, nature center, and canopy walk.

pointsOfInterest.name: Ohio Expo Center and State Fairgrounds
pointsOfInterest.description: 360-acre facility hosting the Ohio State Fair, 185-plus annual events, and year-round exhibitions.

pointsOfInterest.name: Blacklick Creek Greenway Trail
pointsOfInterest.description: 13-mile multi-use trail paralleling Blacklick Creek between Reynoldsburg and Groveport.

pointsOfInterest.name: Eastmoor Civic Association
pointsOfInterest.description: Active neighborhood association organizing community events and civic engagement in Eastmoor.

pointsOfInterest.name: John Glenn Columbus International Airport
pointsOfInterest.description: Central Ohio's primary commercial airport, located on the district's western edge.

pointsOfInterest.name: Walnut Ridge High School
pointsOfInterest.description: Columbus City Schools secondary campus and community anchor for the Walnut Ridge neighborhood.

areaCommissions.name: Northeast Area Commission
areaCommissions.meetingSchedule: Zoning meeting at 6 PM, full commission follows
areaCommissions.meetingLocation: Ohio Dominican University, Peace Hall, 2600 Airport Dr.

snapshots.source: U.S. Census Bureau ACS 5-Year via Census API (backfill_2021.py)

data.data_note: Median income, rent, and home value are population-weighted averages of block-group medians — approximations, not true district medians. See /data/ for methodology.
